behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real filesystem watcher behavior (recursive/subdirectory event delivery), which unit tests and in-process fakes can mask. SYSTEM TRIAD trigger applies because success must be proven via externally visible side effect (file is actually processed after real FS write). Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text: no AI/ML call or multi-agent sequence is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run the daemon against real temp input/output directories. Create a nested subdirectory under the watched root, write a file there, and verify via output/readback (or emitted result artifact) that the file was processed without injecting synthetic watcher events.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: The risk is module handoff/state timing (request intake + dedup window + dispatch), not inherently OS/network/subprocess. Rejected System because behavior text is about a "processing request" and dedup window logic, not a required real infrastructure contract. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text: no AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real dedup + scheduling modules together with controllable clock/time source and test harness inputs. Submit two requests for the same path within the configured window and assert only one dispatch occurs; then advance beyond the window and assert a new request is accepted.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires real OS event semantics (duplicate FS notifications), which mocks often hide. SYSTEM TRIAD trigger applies via externally visible round-trip: one real write must yield exactly one processing result. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running on a real watched directory, perform a single write pattern known to generate multiple FS events on the target OS (or verify multiple raw watcher callbacks occurred). Assert only one processing execution/result artifact is produced for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: Directory deletion/recreation while watch is active is OS-level watcher lifecycle behavior; in-process fakes can miss it. SYSTEM TRIAD trigger applies because correctness is proven only by real round-trip side effect after recreation (new file gets processed). Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon watching a real directory, delete that directory, recreate it at the same path, then place a new file and verify it is processed without restarting the daemon.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering depends on real filesystem metadata/timing and watcher arrival behavior; unit-only checks of sorting logic can give false confidence. SYSTEM TRIAD trigger applies because required outcome is an externally visible processing order observed from real file arrivals. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Use real watched/output directories. Stage multiple files so they are detected in the same arrival window with controlled creation times, then assert observed processing order (e.g., in output/log sequence) matches creation timestamp order.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: This is a real FS timing/partial-write condition requiring OS-level file event and write-completion behavior; mocks can hide race conditions. SYSTEM TRIAD trigger applies through round-trip verification that processing occurs only after complete write and sees final content. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running, start writing a file in chunks while keeping the handle open. Confirm no processing result appears during partial write, then close/finish write and verify processing occurs once with complete final content.
